broker_fee_detail: No broker fee under NYC FARE Act; agent represents landlord (AM Residential)
broker_message: Hi — interested in 666 56th St #4. Could you confirm the floor, whether the half-room/office has a window, the lease term, and full move-in costs? I also saw a few open HPD violations on the building; are those resolved? If the unit shows well I'd like to discuss $1,875. Thanks!
broker_questions: Which floor is unit 4, and is it a full walk-up?
Is the 'half bedroom/office' a legal room with a window and egress?
Is this rent-stabilized? Prior legal rent?
What are the 3 open Class C HPD violations, and are they corrected?
Lease term and are pets allowed?
Are heat/hot water included, any winter outages?
building_risk_rationale: 12 open HPD violations across 16 units = 0.75/unit, marginally BELOW the NYC average of 0.81, so overall load is not alarming. However the mix includes 3 open Class C (immediately hazardous) plus 7 Class B, and the building shows 2 evictions and 1 litigation case. The Class C items in a small 1905 walk-up keep this out of Low; verify whether the 3 C violations are heat/pest related and still open.
commute_notes: N express (Sea Beach) at 8th Ave or R local at 53rd St; 59th St is a key N/R/W transfer. To Midtown ~45-55 min via N. Sunset Park is a hike from Manhattan but has three lines within a 10-min walk.
concessions_to_ask: Landlord corrects open Class C violations before move-in
cons: 3 open Class C violations and an eviction/litigation history
'1.5BR' marketing likely a windowless office room
Prewar walk-up, no floor plan/sqft, undisclosed floor
leverage_notes: Priced mid-pack among comps ($1,775-$2,090) and below the Sunset Park 1BR median asking ($2,349), so limited pure-price leverage. Best leverage is the 3 open Class C violations and undisclosed floor/room-legality — push for a small reduction or repairs.
noise_risks: Dense mixed retail corridor near 5th/8th Aves
Prewar walk-up windows offer little sound insulation
plain_english: A fairly priced, no-fee 1BR in a small prewar Sunset Park walk-up with solid transit. Only listed 5 days, so NOT sitting. The building's overall violation count is normal, but a few immediately-hazardous (Class C) violations and the '1.5BR' spin on what's probably a windowless office are worth verifying on tour. Reasonable to tour and gently negotiate on repairs or ~$50/mo.
pros: No broker fee and priced at/below neighborhood 1BR median
Three subway lines within a 10-minute walk
Building violation load slightly below NYC average

red_flags.how_to_verify: On tour confirm the half-room has a window/egress; ask if it is on the C of O
red_flags.issue: Marketed as '1.5BR' but listed as 1 bed; the 'half bedroom' is an office with likely no legal window
red_flags.why: A windowless interior room cannot legally be a bedroom and inflates perceived value

red_flags.how_to_verify: Request floor plan; measure on tour
red_flags.issue: No square footage, no floor plan, only 6 photos
red_flags.why: Common concealment of small/awkward layout in prewar walk-ups

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask which floor unit 4 is on
red_flags.issue: Walk-up building, unit floor not disclosed (up to 4 stories)
red_flags.why: A 3rd/4th-floor walk-up is a daily burden

red_flags.how_to_verify: Pull HPD Online for the C violations; ask super about resolution
red_flags.issue: 3 open Class C HPD violations in a 16-unit building

sources.label: BMT Sea Beach (N) / 4th Ave (R) near coords

unverified: nature/status of the 3 Class C violations
live HPD total (only open count shown)
